mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in PAYEMS signals rising household income and resilient aggregate demand, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 141679 thousand as of one month before 2015-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward months or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply above its recent trend, investors price a deterioration in labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.5 percent as of one month before 2015-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in RSAFS and HOUST identifies broad household demand and housing-cycle momentum, increasing expected earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and HOUST 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 444926 million dollars and HOUST was 1135 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-HOUST trigger fails to deliver higher 60-day returns than either single-series signal alone in at least 60% of walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates and reduces liquidity available for risk assets, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days after the tightening threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.12 percent as of one month before 2015-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes have non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO below its recent trend signals weakening production volumes and operating leverage, lowering expected earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 105.1018 as of one month before 2015-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, markets price future policy tightening and margin pressure rather than current liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS is below 0.50 percent
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 235.982 and FEDFUNDS was 0.12 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has a non-negative 5-day average return or does not underperform the CPI-only trigger in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with rising RSAFS signals that final demand is translating into current sales, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 16264.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive real demand
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1-RSAFS positive-demand signals fail to produce higher 60-day returns than unconditional returns in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals deteriorating rate-sensitive demand and weaker future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average by more than 10 percent
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1135 thousand units as of two months before 2015-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or average triggered returns exceed baseline.
